commands.review_summary:
This PR adds root and plugin npm shrinkwraps, shrinkwrap generation/check tooling, plugin npm dependency bundling, release/package validation, dependency-review wiring, docs, changelog, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection of PR head shows the release soak deletion, live npm re-resolution path, workspace-only override normalization, unchanged-overlay bundled-dependency bypass, and stale generated versions. I did not run generation or package commands because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer release/security judgment plus author repairs; automation should not independently choose the publishable-lockfile trust model or remove release validation.

target.title: chore: harden npm shrinkwrap release path
target.body:
## Summary
- Ship the root OpenClaw npm package and every OpenClaw-owned publishable npm plugin with generated `npm-shrinkwrap.json` files.
- Generate shrinkwrap from the published npm dependency graph, strip dev deps, and apply `pnpm-workspace.yaml` override policy.
- Add shrinkwrap and `package-lock.json` to the dependency security review surface through CODEOWNERS, dependency-change awareness, labeler sizing, and dependency report output.
- Enforce root shrinkwrap in release/package validators, check plugin-local shrinkwrap before plugin pack/publish, and document the root/plugin contract.
- Bundle plugin runtime dependencies for OpenClaw-owned npm plugin packages, including skipped optional native packages needed by other operating systems.

## Explainer
`npm-shrinkwrap.json` is npm's publishable lockfile. Unlike `package-lock.json`, npm can include it in published packages, so installing `openclaw` or an OpenClaw-owned npm plugin can resolve through the reviewed dependency graph that release tooling generated.

This does not replace `pnpm-lock.yaml` for source development. The repo still uses pnpm as the source-of-truth package manager. The generator derives npm shrinkwrap from each package's stock published dependency graph: root `openclaw` for the CLI/Gateway package, and `extensions/<id>/package.json` for each `publishToNpm` plugin package.

Plugin installs already run through npm's managed root, so third-party packages that ship shrinkwrap are supported by npm normally. This PR makes the OpenClaw-owned plugin packages ship that lockfile too, and keeps plugin-only dependency graphs plugin-local instead of pretending the root package shrinkwrap covers them.

Because lockfiles can redirect resolved package versions and tarballs, this PR treats `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, and `package-lock.json` diffs as security-review surfaces. The package path rejects accidental `package-lock.json`, requires shrinkwrap for new root tarballs, and checks generated shrinkwraps have no dev dependency graph.

Bundled plugin packages use `bundledDependencies` at pack/publish time so npm installs can succeed from the reviewed tarball without resolving plugin runtime dependencies from the registry. Optional native dependencies are forced into the packed dependency closure when npm skips them for the build host, so a Linux-built Codex plugin tarball still carries the Darwin, Linux, and Windows Codex native packages.

## Verification
- `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:generate`
- `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check`
- `bash scripts/plugin-npm-publish.sh --pack-dry-run extensions/amazon-bedrock | jq -r '.[0].files[].path' | grep '^npm-shrinkwrap.json$'`
- `NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/tmp/openclaw-npm-cache npm pack --dry-run --ignore-scripts --json`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-changes-report.test.ts test/scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.test.ts test/openclaw-npm-release-check.test.ts test/package-scripts.test.ts test/package-manager-config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/package-manager-config.test.ts test/plugin-npm-package-manifest.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-changes-report.test.ts test/package-scripts.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/install-source-utils.test.ts src/infra/npm-install-env.test.ts test/plugin-npm-package-manifest.test.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.install.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.list.test.ts src/plugins/install-overrides.test.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-records.test.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-store.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/plugins/uninstall.test.ts test/plugin-npm-runtime-build.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check`
- `actionlint .github/workflows/dependency-change-awareness.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml`
- `npm pack openclaw@2026.4.25 --json --pack-destination <tmp> && node scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.mjs <tmp>/*.tgz`
- macOS: Linux-built bundled Codex tarball installed offline from an empty npm cache, then `codex exec` returned `OPENCLAW-LINUXPACK-FIXED-MAC-PREFLIGHT`.
- Windows: AWS Crabbox native Windows `run_5ee7b23beb09` / `cbx_00058c590587` packed Codex, Slack, and WhatsApp, installed all three offline from an empty npm cache, verified all Codex native packages, and completed a live Codex turn with `OPENCLAW-WINDOWS-BUNDLE-PROOF`.
- Linux: AWS Crabbox `run_dd4bcff36869` / `cbx_255f19b13dcc` packed Codex, Slack, WhatsApp, and Brave, installed all four with `NPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true` from an empty npm cache, and reached `multi_plugin_offline_install=pass`.
- Linux Docker ARM64: `OPENCLAW_PLUGIN_NPM_BUNDLE_DEPENDENCIES=1 OPENCLAW_CODEX_NPM_PLUGIN_MODEL=codex/gpt-5.4 bash scripts/e2e/codex-npm-plugin-live-docker.sh` passed Codex CLI preflight plus three OpenClaw local agent turns through the npm-installed Codex plugin.
- Publish dry-run: `npm publish --dry-run --ignore-scripts /tmp/openclaw-linux-pack-docker-fixed2/openclaw-codex-2026.5.20.tgz` accepted the 561.7 MB packed / 1.5 GB unpacked Codex tarball and failed only because version `2026.5.20` already exists.
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OpenClaw root npm installs and OpenClaw-owned npm plugin installs now carry publishable shrinkwrap files, while lockfile/shrinkwrap changes are treated as security-review surfaces. OpenClaw-owned npm plugin tarballs also bundle their runtime dependency closure, including cross-platform optional native packages that npm normally skips on the build host.

Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout; native AWS Windows; AWS Linux; local Docker Linux ARM64; npm 11; pnpm 11; empty npm-cache offline plugin installs; multiple plugin installs in one managed npm root; live Codex CLI and OpenClaw agent turns through the npm-installed Codex plugin.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Generated and checked root plus 30 plugin shrinkwraps; packed bundled Codex, Slack, WhatsApp, and Brave plugin tarballs; installed Codex, Slack, WhatsApp, and Brave from `npm-pack:` with `NPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true`; verified Codex tarballs include Darwin, Linux, and Windows native Codex packages; ran focused tests for release/tarball/dependency-review/plugin-package/install surfaces; ran Docker Linux ARM64 live Codex npm-plugin E2E.

Evidence after fix: Root dry-run pack includes `npm-shrinkwrap.json` and excludes `package-lock.json`; generated plugin shrinkwraps exist for all 30 `publishToNpm` plugin packages; selected generated shrinkwraps have 0 dev packages; Codex tarballs include `@openai/codex`, `@openai/codex-darwin-arm64`, `@openai/codex-darwin-x64`, `@openai/codex-linux-arm64`, `@openai/codex-linux-x64`, `@openai/codex-win32-arm64`, and `@openai/codex-win32-x64`; Windows and Linux offline installs created npm-pack install records for multiple plugins.

Observed result after fix: Release/package checks and focused tests pass; macOS, Windows, and Linux offline plugin installs work; Linux Docker ARM64 live Codex E2E passed with CLI preflight and three OpenClaw local agent turns; npm publish dry-run handles the large bundled Codex tarball and reports only the expected already-published version conflict.

What was not tested: Actual npm/plugin publish and full release CI. AWS Linux live Codex turn was not rerun after the multi-plugin install because two later AWS leases failed to open SSH; Linux live coverage is from the Docker ARM64 E2E lane.
